scenario: difficulty setting boundaries with an overbearing parent

turns.user_message: I have this issue with my parent. They treat me like I'm still a teenager and I'm 32. Every conversation turns into them telling me what to do.
I know, right? It sounds simple when I say it out loud but it doesn't feel simple.
It's been going on for a while, honestly. I just kept pushing it aside.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with an overbearing parent and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
That's a good question. Let me think... I guess I feel like I should have this figured out by now.
I don't know. Some days are better than others. Today is a harder day.
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with an overbearing parent is never going to get better.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting an overbearing parent control my life more than I realized.
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by an overbearing parent more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
My my sibling noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
I tried to talk about this with my sibling but they just don't get it. They think I should just just get over it and move on.
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about an overbearing parent and I should just deal with it.
I had a moment this week where I had a moment of clarity where everything felt manageable for once. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with an overbearing parent is never going to get better.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
I had a moment this week where I had a moment of clarity where everything felt manageable for once. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
What you just said made me feel something. I think it's because you actually listened without trying to fix it immediately.
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about an overbearing parent and I should just deal with it.
I tried to talk about this with my sibling but they just don't get it. They think I should just just get over it and move on.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Maybe I'm just not strong enough to deal with this.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
Something shifted just now. I feel a little more grounded, like my feet are touching the floor again. I don't know why.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life an overbearing parent has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
You know what really gets me? nobody in my life seems to understand how hard this actually is. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life an overbearing parent has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.
I feel like I've been carrying this for so long. It's like carrying a backpack full of rocks that nobody else can see.
You know what really gets me? nobody in my life seems to understand how hard this actually is. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
Thank you for letting me just... be messy about this. I needed that.
Thank you for letting me just... be messy about this. I needed that.
I feel lighter. Not great, but lighter. Does that make sense?
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
